title: thymus serpyllum subsp. ligusticus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. ligusticus, also known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a low-growing, perennial herb with tiny leaves and small, fragrant flowers, commonly found in dry, rocky areas and often used as a medicinal plant. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. ligusticus include its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, wild thyme is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be added to foods and skincare products due to its antiseptic and antioxidant properties, providing various health benefits.
